EMIG PRO ODI V2 LOCK-ON GRIPS:
 
 The signature grip of 5-Time AMA National and Supercross Champion, Jeff Emig. Designed by Jeff and developed and manufactured in-house in USA at ODI Grips. The choice of pro motocross riders who want that soft compound for improved comfort and less hand fatigue. 
 
 EMIG PRO V2 LOCK-ON GRIP FEATURES:
 
 The Emig Pro V2 Signature Grip is developed with our Pro Soft-Compound
 
 Slim diameter profile with diamond knurled pattern to improve traction and vibration damping (Ends: 29.5mm Diameter. Center: 31mm Diameter)
 
 Ergonomic design conforms to riders hand without feeling bulky
 
 Provides extra padding for big impacts and hard hits
 
 PRO COMPOUND - Soft Pillow-Top knurls for extra-comfort and control
 
 ALUMINUM REINFORCED ENDS - Provide extra durability against grip blowout
 
 Designed to fit 7/8” Size Handlebar Controls
 
 110mm Grip Length / 125mm overall length with Clamp
 
 ODI V2 LOCK-ON GRIP FEATURES:
 
 100% Slip-Free Performance Guaranteed
 
 Lock-On Grip provides simple installation and removal
 
 3mm Allen ensures solid twist-free mounting without waiting for glue to dry
 
 No safety wire required means longer grip life, even in the event of a crash
 
 Available in 11 amazing colorways
 
 Throttle Tube Included with Snap-On Throttle Cams to match most modern MX bikes
 
 @odigrips

Moto-bred. V-Twin ready. The legendary ODI Ruffian full-knurl grip is now purpose-built for V-Twin performance, featuring ODI’s patented Lock-On system, slim 33mm profile, and proprietary medium-compound TPE for unmatched control, feedback, and traction. No glue. No slip. Just pure connection.
 
 -Moto-inspired performance grip
 -Full-knurl diamond pattern
 -Lock-On billet clamp (T15 Torx)
 -TBW-specific throttle tube
 -Slim feel riders & racers love
 -Proudly Made in the USA 
 
 Available for multiple styles of Harley-Davidson motorcycles. Built for riders who demand precision—on the street or on the limiter.
 
 #ODIgrips #RuffianGrips #HarleyDavidson #MadeInUSA #HotBike

Now it's the turn of our MTB grips to be awarded the #1 Grips (and for the 14th Year in a row) in the @vitalmtb audience survey! We are truly thankful for everyone who took the time to vote for us. 👏 
 
 40+ years of being at the forefront of grip design and with the most models and colors. We invented the Lock-On system and just about every grip design out there! 
 
 ODI stands for OBSERVE - DESIGN - INNOVATE
 
 #odigrips #odi #grips #vitalmtb #mtb #bike #numberone #lockon

WOW! For the 9th Year in a Row we are the #1 Grips in the @vitalmx audience survey! Thank you to all who voted for us 👏 
 
 We pride ourselves in producing the very best grips to make your ride more enjoyable, with unique, performance driven designs, advanced materials and with as many fitment options as possible to keep ODI at the forefront of the market.
 
 For over 40 years we have been the World Leader in Grip Technology and currently produce all types including Lock-Ons (we invented them), Universal Lock-Ons, Slip-Ons and our range has the largest selection of patterns available.
 
 With the recent launch of the NOMAD Lock-On grips we have taken another step forward in the evolution of grips by collaborating with @d3olab to produce a grip that offers 2 x more vibration damping resulting in less arm pump so you can ride longer and farther without fatigue setting in.
 
 #odigrips #grips #odi #moto #mx #vitalmx #numberone

The 2024 season was nothing short of extraordinary for the Australian Factory Honda Racing Team, with the team sweeping every championship class. But with success comes expectation. In 2025, the bar has been set even higher, and the target on our back has never been clearer.

 MX1 – Defending champion Kyle Webster returns aboard the Boost Mobile Honda CRF450RWE, with Wilson Todd joining him in the premier class on the REPCO Honda.
 MX2 – Brodie Connolly is back to defend his title on the Polyflor Honda CRF250RWE, alongside Alex Larwood, who returns for his second season with National Finance and Loans Honda.
 MX3 – Rising star Kayd Kingsford represents Pro Honda Oils Honda.
 Women’s MX – Four-time champion Charli Cannon is ready to fight for another title on the SCT Logistics CRF250RWE.

⭐️NEW PRODUCT⭐️ Introducing the ODI MUD KNUCKLE KIT 😎 Designed to mount to the front of any motocross grip for added control in WET/MUDDY conditions. A necessity for those pouring down, rainy days at the track to enable mechanical traction between your already installed gripset and the inside of your 1” row of knuckles. Each Mud Knuckle is injection molded in-house at ODI and features Safety-Wire Slots to perfectly secure to the front side of your grips. Improves traction in extremely wet and/or muddy conditions. A must for any toolbox while heading to the races in the Winter months. 
 
 The kit includes: 
 2 x Mud Knuckle injection moulded traction strips (Length 100mm/4”).
 6 x 6” strands of ODI Safety Wire. 
 
 2 x World Champion and 5 x USA National Enduro Champion @stewardbaylor514 with the lowdown 🤩 
 
 Can also be used for DH Mtb when conditions are wet and muddy!

Our new VANQUISH grips made it onto the front page of Pinkbike! Let's address some things raised in the comments: 
 
 As @mikekazimer points out: This is a BRAND NEW material (D3O® GRIP™ compound) that is completely DIFFERENT from the material D3O® uses in their knee pads and protection. GRIP™ compound has been specifically developed to reduce vibration (making it perfect for grips), whereas the material used in their knee pads and protection is developed to absorb impacts as it is viscoelastic and stiffens under impact.
 
 Our v2.1 Lock-On System technology is the world's most secure grip system. We are able to use thinner clamps as all 360 degrees of the inside surface of the alu clamp is in contact with the handlebar (metal on metal) giving greater clamping force. This system makes the clamps strong enough on their own and doesn't require additional hold anywhere else on the grips.
 
 Orange is D3O®'s brand color hence the use of it as accents on the grips.
 
 If you have any more questions then pop them in the comments below and we will do our best to answer them!

NEW! VANQUISH v2.1 Lock-On Grips made with proprietary D3O® GRIP™ material.
 
 This collaboration came about as ODI was the obvious choice for D3O® to design a grip that would allow riders to best feel the anti-vibration properties of their new GRIP™ compound. Experience gained through decades of being the World Leader in Grip Technology was utilised in designing the Vanquish grips.
 
 Vanquish grips are designed around our unique v2.1 Lock-On System which is the most secure system available for fitting grips to handlebars.
 
 To allow the D3O® GRIP™ material to shine we undertook extensive testing, in different grip designs, with riders of all levels, and used the findings to develop the design of the grips. 
 
 One of the unique design features is a brand new take on our waffle design allows the rider to stay connected to the grip without having to grip tightly. 
 
 Double-reinforced grip ends for even better durability.
 
 Colors: Black/Orange, Graphite/Orange & Orange/Black.
 
 Outside Diameter: 32.75mm

Ok 2-stroke fans! Here it is…
 
 *video link is in my bio
 
 This 300sx sat for months after Loretta’s last summer. The final two motos were a mess, it rained and rained. Possibly two of the worst mud motos I’ve ever raced in my 45 years of racing MX. 
 
 So I teamed up with Whiskey Throttle Media, and we decided to do a bike “re-build” on this machine and get it back to a race-ready condition. 
 
 I’ve already read some comments on some of the previous posts about this video, trust me, the bike looked OK but there was mud, dirt and sand in every crevice of this machine, and there’s no way I was going to race it without the cases being split, and everything gone through first. 
 
 I want to thank Luc Santos from Factory MX Vacations for doing all the hard work with the tear-down and assembly. 
 
 Jamie Ellis and his team from Twisted Development worked their asses off and pulled 20% more HP’s than stock out of this engine. AND, it’s built to last…
 •TD mapping, and engine mods
 •FMF exhaust 
 •Moto Tassinari VForce 4 reeds
 •Hinson Clutch
 •VP T2 racing fuel
 •DT1 air filter by MXFS
 
 Of course I only run ENZO Suspension mods. The spring fork conversion is as good as it gets for the paying customer. 
 
 My father Gary made me a custom set of EMIG RACING clamps
 
 As always, I run the ODI Podium Flight bars in the RC4 bend, along with the black/white EMIG PRO V2 lock-on grips. 
 
 Dubya USA rebuilt the wheelsets, gotta be gold on gold!
 •Haan Hubs
 •Excel A60 rims
 
 Chrome Dirt Tricks sprockets w stock gearing. Then linked up a gold Regina chain. 
 
 Pirelli provided a set of Scorpion MX32 MID SOFT tires. So good👌🏼👌🏼👌🏼
 
 Throttle Syndicate redesigned the graphics and seat cover. Acerbis provided the additional plastics. 
 •Frame guards
 •Skid plate
 
 I went with ASV levers for the first time and loved the feel. Black and gold. 💁🏻‍♂️
 
 Last thing we did we throw on some Titanium Pro-pegs. 
 
 Fortunate enough to work with the best companies in the business and I would like to sincerely thank them for all their hard work that went into this machine. Braaap!

The Tennessee National track was sick! Was feeling good on the bike all weekend but took me longer than usual to start understanding the track and start picking up the pace. I had much more in me this weekend but just made too many mistakes when it counted. Blew off into the tape twice, came to a stop, and somehow broke my chain in the Redneck Ravine during my race run. Considering all that 8th was an okay finish but definitely not all I had in me. Was a learning experience for sure and I can’t wait to get back between the tape 🫡

 Jim Monro and Dennis Luffman flew South to NZ, where the sun shines, the dirt is good and the vibes are all time! The boys took full advantage of some of the best riding in the world while catching up with a huge crew, sending super-sized jumps and railing the dusty corners of Queenstown before hitting the skatepark… and the odd party!
 
 Half a world away there’s still a Bike Park to run so of course Dan Atherton wintered in the Dyfi! Shred Mansion’s Alf Raynor and Alfie Heming stayed to help, braving the Welsh weather to keep the tracks in top condition … but when the sun came out so did the prototype Atherton S170s for some serious abuse!

This past weekend, Team Sherco 1's Cody Webb and Cooper Abbott flew across the pond to take on what is arguably the hardest and most well know enduro in the world, the Red Bull Erzberg Rodeo in Austria 🇦🇹 
  
 More than 1100 people enter this hare scramble, 600 make the final day. Cody and Cooper both qualified on row one of the top 50. 
  
 Cody was one of only 17 finishers and the only American finisher. He finished p10 on his SE 300 Factory. Cooper would make it through the "Carl's Dinner" checkpoint, this is the most famous rock section, and finish 37th overall. The team spoke to Cody during the race, he said this was by far the most difficult edition of the race in the years he has raced it. The race changes year to year because the mine is still active which changes the course 🏁

@huttenmetaalyamaharacing EMX250's rider @andreabonacorsi35 has extended his European Championship lead with a remarkable round victory at the third round of the EMX250 Championship in Agueda, Portugal.
  
 Bonacorsi continued his impressive 2023 EMX250 title charge with a series of spectacular passes as he charged from fifth to win the opening race of the weekend by 7 seconds. 
 
 In the second and final EMX250 race in Portugal, Bonacorsi displayed his champion spirit with a heroic ride to third, despite not having a clutch lever after another rider collided with him on lap-2. 
  
 The Italian powerhouse has yet to finish outside the top-three this season, and now enjoys a comfortable 35-point lead in the series. 
  
 The Hutten Metaal Yamaha Official EMX250 team will now travel 500 kilometers east to Madrid, Spain, where the next round of the EMX250 Championship will take place in less than a week’s time – May 6th and 7th.

🌟NEW!🌟ODI REFLEX v2.1 Lock-On Grips – Superior Comfort and Control! 🌟
 
 We are excited to announce the newest addition to the ODI line up of v2.1 Lock-On Grips – the REFLEX grip. Drawing on over 40 years of experience of producing performance-driven grips and feedback from our extensive network of top riders across the globe, the REFLEX grips have been engineered to reduce impacts and vibration being passed on to the riders’ hands to allow you to ride more with less pain and fatigue.
 
 REFLEX grips are the latest innovation in impact damping to keep you riding longer and farther as they’ve been specifically engineered to reduce vibration. Like having suspension for your hands without compromising control. 
 
 Two sizes: 
 REFLEX 33.5mm Diameter / REFLEX XL 34.5mm Diameter

ODI Grips has teamed up with @connorfields11 to create the CFP Cash For Podiums program. This is what Connor has to say:
 
 “I thought long and hard about how I wanted to give back to the next generation of pros, and I am super excited about what ODI and I came up with. The CFP Cash For Podiums program is here. 
 
 As a junior elite, it is so exciting getting your first paychecks from racing. While making a few bucks is exciting, the harsh realities of transitioning to racing at the pro level begin to show. It is hard, expensive, and finding support isn’t always easy. 
 
 I want to motivate, inspire, and help the next generation of pros continue to move forward by paying them for podium finishes at the UCI rounds”.
 
 Apply today! Limited spots available, Application link in bio.

Connor Fields and @odigrips have teamed up to support the next generation of racers via the CFP (Cash For Podiums) rider program and are now taking applications for a limited number of spots. The program is open to UCI Junior and U23 Men and Women. Applications will be reviewed by @connorfields11 and only a limited number of athletes who will be eligible for Payments for Podium finishes at UCI races. More info and applications available by going to odigrips.com or checking the link in bio.

5 years in a row! The @slr_honda team of @slamuels130 @jmorgan403 and @kendallnorman once again won the Pro Moto Unlimited class at the Baja 1000 🇲🇽 to make it a five-peat! 🏆 Their Honda CRF450 was specced with ODI RC4 Podium Flight Handlebars and ODI White Pro-Soft Full Waffle Grips. Go checkout our website for all the details - Link In Bio.
 
 Nostalgic…legendary…epic… however you want to describe the BAJA 1000 race we all know it’s just a wild classic race south of the border. Fans, families, locals and transplants from around the world come down to the beautiful Baja California to witness the spectacle and the SLR Team certainly produced the goods.
 
 Race Report: 
 
 Justin Morgan would start off the race at 3:06am, in 7th place. Making up time by picking brilliant lines he made his way up to 2nd place by race mile 275. Late on in the day he would pick up the last hand-off from Kendall to take the bike and cross the line for a well-deserved victory making it 5 years in a row for SLR Honda
 
 Mark Samuels was second on the bike and he would begin his leg in 2nd place having to reel in first place with the gap of 15 minutes. In 100 miles he had closed the gap to 12 minutes. The lead bike then had issues so he passed to be for overall first to hand-off to Kendall.
 
 Kendall Norman took over his portion of the ride with the pressure of a lead to maintain and to secure the distance without issues. He raced down the iconic ocean line sprint towards El Alamo and at race mile 725 he made the final hand-off to Justin to take it home.
 
 #odigrips #odi #moto #baja1000 #slrhonda #slamliferacing #honda #crf450 #mx #mexico #baja #california

NEW VIDEO! The Chill Trail was born out of the desire to integrate a trail into a bike park that would
 translate an athlete's vision into a trail that everyone could ride. For many years @vinny_t_ has spent a lot of time in the forest digging for new trails for his videos. He has built many times in many different environments almost every module that can exist which gives him a solid experience in the field.
 
 The project started in the spring of 2021 and will not be finished until July 2022. Like a fine wine, a track takes time to reveal its full potential. It's not just digging a track, it's testing it, correcting the curves, adapting the passages to the environment in order to arrive at a track that allows you to fully exploit your potential on the bike. But we don't all have the potential of Vinny T.
 
 "Many months of work went into the construction of this new track. I am very happy with the result, digging in the forest is as important as riding in my eyes, one cannot go without the other. The fact that the trail is not ephemeral and can be used by everyone is what I really like. The Chill Trail project was born from the collaboration between Bike Vision and Vinny T. I thank again Seb Giraldi, all the Bikevision team, and those who came to help us!" - Vinny T
 
 Chill Trail 🎥 Link In Bio
 
 📷 @maxime_rambaud

The iconic dust and rock of the 45th State is no stranger to having Belgian freeride athlete @thomasgenon,aka Tommy G, tearing down its shale slopes and launching off its cliff faces. But while Tommy has visited Utah on eight separate occasions, all of these trips have been centred around riding in one of the wildest sporting events on the planet. Red Bull Rampage.
 
 ‘No Schedule’ is a video concept that builds off the back of Tommy’s multiple Rampage experiences. The project sets out to answer a question: Away from the structure and time constraints of contest riding, what can a rider like Tommy do with the legendary Utah terrain? To find an answer to this, the project sees Tommy head out to the desert on a 3 week trip with good friends @louisreboul @eliottlapotre and @paulfisson. With videographer @christianrigal 🎥 capturing the madness.
 
 With endless style, insane moves, experimental features and epic scenery, ‘No Schedule’ achieves all it set out to do.

Go support @ur_team with their "Ride 4 Ukraine" campaign 🙌The racing season is ramping up, summer is around the corner, the fans are back at the races but for us it’s hard to ignore that this weekend in Leogang we will be racing about 900km from the Ukraine border, where the country sees an unfair and dramatic situation for millions of people.
  
 As @ns_bikesco are from Poland, a country bordering Ukraine, they started a “Bikes not bombs” T-shirt in support of the Ukrainian people. Inspired by them, they decided to also show their support by making a Ukrainian color themed jersey that will be auctioned.
 
 These custom jerseys will be worn and raced by George, Kye and Vanesa then they will be raffled off with all donations going towards “The Polish National Campaign To Support Ukraine.” which are directly helping as many Ukrainian people touched by this situation.
 
 They have created a sub-campaign which is giving directly all the donations to the main fundraise. 
 
 Via the link in the @ur_team bio you can donate whatever you want and they will randomly draw 6 people to be able to win the jerseys which will be announced through their instagram. When donating please leave your full name otherwise you won't be entered into the raffle of the jersey.

Godspeed, Marty Smith. April 27, 2022 marks the 2 year anniversary of the passing of one of the greats, Marty Smith. Marty was the epitome of American Motocross, with his long hair surf style and get it done attitude. 
  
 Smith retired from Motocross in 1981 but later on started teaching Motocross in his MX Clinics to the next generation of riders. 
  
 Legendary 3-time AMA Motocross Champion, Marty Smith developed the Re-Grip with ODI to help riders position their hands properly on the bars and to remail in the attack position. The Marty Smith Re-Grip is manufactured here at ODI in our California manufacturing facility out of the same amazing materials and compounds that we have been known for.

RAIN OR SHINE: As they prep for the 2022 DH race season, local Race Face and @cyclesdevinci Development Racing riders Patrick Laffey and Drew Mozell dodge the rain, snow, and windstorms of Vancouver Island to tackle some rough off-season Prevost laps in their latest video, "Rain or Shine." 
  
 Travelling through the deep greens of their local trails for some fast and slick riding paired with some quirky hijinks is really all these young racers are about. While racing is always primary in their minds, taking the time to just ride your bike for fun makes the hard work and sacrifices feel worthwhile. 
  
 Thank you to all the volunteers, individuals, and groups, past and present, that maintain and continue to elevate the trails on Mount Prevost.

NOW LIVE! Luke Whitlock's “No Surrender” is a MUST WATCH!
 ​
 @lukewhitlock_, born and raised in Reno, Nevada has been turning heads with his rough and rowdy approach to Freeriding recently. He was inspired by the OG Freeriders and this is his "tip of the hat" to the gritty riding style of the "New World Disorder" days. Luke’s determination and drive was instilled into him at a young age while he was working for his father at the family painting business. If Luke doesn’t have a paintbrush in his hand, you’ll find him on his bike everywhere from home in Reno to the Utah desert appreciating the solitude and vastness while carving out never before ridden features.

🚨Product Spotlight🚨 The Emig Pro V2 Lock-On Grip was one of the most detailed MX Grips that we have designed to this day. Check out some of the key features this grip offers: 
 
 • Raised center JE logo pattern conforms to palms, improves padding & reduces vibration
 •Extra-soft, undercut independent ribs deliver lateral & rotational traction
 •Alloy reinforced end caps provide extra durability against grip blowout
 •ODI exclusive locking clutch-side collar attaches the grip providing 360° of Security
 •Simple Installation and Removal Without Waiting for Glue to Dry
 •Throttle Tube Included with Snap On Cam to match more models
 •No safety wire required means longer grip life, even in the event of a crash
 •Kit provides Cams for both 2 & 4-Stroke Applications

#FactoryFriday – @aarongwin1 takes you through some of the technical innovations involved with the AG2 Grips & how these innovations benefit you as the rider!
  
 Our initial partnership with Aaron produced numerous innovations that pushed grip technology to new levels. With the new AG2 Grips, we wanted to take things even further. We started with an ultra soft pro compound and built this into an improved knurl pattern that provides extra tackiness and control along with reduced vibration transfer. We coupled this with our exclusive offset design that offers even more impact dampening where it is needed most, without increasing the grip diameter. We then redesigned the most impact prone portions of the grips to provide and even better feel and accurate control when riding.

 Post your video to your feed and use hashtag #ODIGRIPFLIPCHALLENGE and call out the name of the grips in your post for your chance to be featured on the @odigrips Instagram story as well as a cool swag bag of accessories and your favorite pair of grips. A winner will be selected each day this weekend: Fri, Sat, & Sun
 Good luck!!!!
